Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Against Defendant Safeway, Inc., Under C.C.P. Section 2030.290; Request For $2,060.00 In Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Safeway, Inc., And/Or Its Attorneys Of Record, Wolfe & Wyman, Llp, Pursuant Ccp 2023.010 Et Seq. And 2025.010 Et Seq.; Memorandum Of Points And Authorites; Declaration Of Kevin S. Conlogue In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-01-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS BLANCA MENDEZ, IDUAL, AND O. M.'s Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Against Defendant Safeway, Inc., Under C.C.P. Section 2030.290; Request For $2,060.00 In Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Safeway, Inc., And/Or Its Attorneys Of Record, Wolfe & Wyman, Llp, Pursuant Ccp 2023.010 Et Seq. And 2025.010 Et Seq.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed motion to compel defendant Safeway, Inc. to verify its responses to Plaintiff's Special Interrogatories, Set Two, is granted. Safeway shall provide verifications within 20 days of entry of this order. The court grants the unopposed request for sanctions in the amount of $2060 and the unopposed request to make them payable jointly and severally by Safeway and its attorneys of record, Wolfe & Wyman LLP. Sanctions shall be paid within 20 days of entry of this order.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

2 - PLAINTIFF BLANCA MENDEZ, and ODRICS MEMBRENO's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Safeway, Inc.S Person(S) Most Qualified And Employees And Request For Production Of Documents (First Amended)

See 12/23/24 order on the Blanca Medez "Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway, Inc.S Person(S) Most Qualified And Employees To Attend And Testify At A Deposition And To Produce The Documents."

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway, Inc.S Person(S) Most Qualified And Employees To Attend And Testify At A Deposition And To Produce The Documents
Off Calendar Dec-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Kevin Conlogue, Esq. 213-255-8837 for Plaintiff.
Terrence Brash, Esq. 925-708-7000 Wolfe & Wyman LLP for Defendant Safeway.
Brian Gregory, Esq. for new Defendant King Retail Solutions, Inc.
Julie Batz, Esq. for Defendant Equity One (west coast portfolio)

8:54am At the instruction of the Court, the clerk instructs the parties to meet and confer re: the deadline/compliance date.
9:17am Parties report they have stipulated to 1/31/25.

1 - PLAINTIFFs BLANCA MENDEZ, and O. M.'s Motion To Compel Defendant Safeway, Inc.S Person(S) Most Qualified And Employees To Attend And Testify At A Deposition And To Produce The Documents.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with a modification on the compliance date of 1/31/25 per stipulation of the parties.

Plaintiff Blanca Mendez's motion to compel deposition of Defendant Safeway, Inc.'s person(s) most qualified and employees and request for production of documents to which no opposition has been received is GRANTED.

Plaintiff has demonstrated that the discovery at issue is are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Defendant Safeway, Inc. has not demonstrated a meritorious objection. Good cause appearing, the court orders Defendant SAFEWAY, INC. to produce the PMQs and employees as noticed and appear via videoconference at 8383 Wilshire Blvd., Ste. 822, Beverly Hills, California, by 1/31/25 per stipulation of parties at the hearing, for their deposition, along with the production of documents as requested.

Defendant Safeway, Inc.'s actions with respect to the subject discovery are without substantial justification. Defendant SAFEWAY, INC. and its counsel of record, Wolfe & Wyman, LLP, must pay sanctions in the amount of $1,200 to Plaintiff by 1/31/25 per stipulation of parties at the hearing.

Plaintiff is advised courtesy copies must be properly fastened. Papers stapled in the middle of the page are not helpful and, in the future, may be disregarded.

A proposed order is submitted for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
